title: Update images
body:
Revert temporary workaround commit and update all VM images.

***Depends on***: #11164, #11166

comments.body: A quick scan and the changes look OK, but the test system is an ugly red....

> OK, but the test system is an ugly red

Super-ugly actually...[I wonder if the SELinux issue](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10709#issuecomment-871590903) really isn't fixed yet.  I think I'll let this PR sit until after the holiday week, then re-build VM images, cross my fingers, and hope.
I'm back now, started fresh build of VM images for CI.
Ref: https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/82
@cevich Is this still valid?

> @cevich Is this still valid?

Yep, it would be nice for consistency sake.  More importantly it's not good for us to continue w/ giuseppe's workaround (different Ubuntu vs Fedora images) long-term.  I'll look at the failures tomorrow and see what's what (we were waiting on an update to container-selinux or some other selinux related packages in Fedora).

[Fedora 33](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6138786426912768/html/int-podman-fedora-33-root-host.log.html#t--podman-exec---privileged-with-user--1):

    Error: open /dev/dma_heap: permission denied

[Fedora 34 and Ubuntu](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6244339543179264/html/int-remote-ubuntu-2010-root-host.log.html):

    Failed to decode the keys [\"secret\" \"secret.opts\"] from \"/usr/share/containers/containers.conf\".

Error: open /dev/dma_heap: permission

Looks like an out of date selinux policy?
Latest is supposed to be: selinux-policy-3.14.6-38.fc33

Failed to decode the keys [\"secret\" \"secret.opts\"] from \"/usr/share/containers/containers.conf\".

Needs an updated containers-common.  But this is not supposed to cause a failure.
I am updating the containers-common package in f34 with the correct secrets config options.
container-selinux-2.163.0-2.fc33 is needed to fix this.

> Error: open /dev/dma_heap: permission
> Looks like an out of date selinux policy?
> Latest is supposed to be: selinux-policy-3.14.6-38.fc33

For F33, I don't have the policy package version at hand, but we're using `container-selinux-2.160.2-1.fc33-noarch`, so it sounds like that's too old anyway.  Let me see if I can side-load in FEDORA-2021-862d1936a6 and FEDORA-2021-01325d81c4 into VMs manually, confirm they address some issues, and then I can also give karma...

Note: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10848 is now skipping "bud with --runtime and --runtime-flag" of the buildah-bud tests.  Once the images are updated, we can revert that in test/buildah-bud/apply-podman-deltas.

> Once the images are updated, we can revert that in test/buildah-bud/apply-podman-deltas.

Oh gosh, I will be the first one to completely forget to do that.  Let me  add a commit here to take care of it...

Manual testing results:

* On `int podman fedora-34 root host tests` + fully updated VM + [FEDORA-2021-01325d81c4](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2021-01325d81c4) the situation is greatly improved.  Only a few tests flaked.
* On `int podman fedora-33 root host` + fully updated VM + [FEDORA-2021-862d1936a6](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2021-862d1936a6) I did *not* see a noticeable improvement.  The `dma_heap` permissions errors seem equally as plentiful :disappointed:
@rhatdan would you mind poking me when the selinux-policy package update is ready for F33? I'm happy to test it / give bodhi karma.
@zpytela @wrabcak Why does f33 still have the dma_heap issue?  What version of selinux-policy fixes this?

F33 has less users, so it gets to stable after 2 weeks, not with karma up:
https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2021-3b341e9e71
Oh great, thanks, I can test that...
...confirmed (what ya'll already knew), FEDORA-2021-3b341e9e71 does fix the dma_heap problem on F33.

Current status: We're waiting on FEDORA-2021-01325d81c4 and FEDORA-2021-862d1936a6 to make it into updates/updates-testing then I can rebuild images and it should resolve most of the current test failures here (Ubuntu is still a question-mark)
(trying an experimental set of images...not fit for actual use, just testing them)

Whoa, what a long journey for what should have been a simple image update!

Current status: New Fedora images are forthcoming with all the necessary updated packages (I think).  We've also got some needed updates in-process on the Ubuntu side (so I'll need to rebuild those again).
@lsm5 FYI/Reminder: When this PR merges, it means CI is passing with "that" package set in all VMs.  Likely a fine time to move kubik:testing -> stable.

@giuseppe when you get a moment, I'm seeing something I thought you fixed, in `int podman fedora-33 root container` task, `podman run container with systemd PID1` test (so, `--cgroup-manager cgroupfs` and `--runtime runc`):

`Error: unable to load cgroup at /machine.slice/libpod-55a1ab319eb117555ef7be4bd69b014cab59356646effec7a2a7aaaa8208b516.scope/libpod_parent/libpod-e01b21e01ec3298ef8ff444c0b2430057e2f32438125f145f69ad6e2f79baf70: cgroup deleted
`

Annotated log link: https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6161651574505472/html/int-podman-fedora-33-root-container.log.html#t--podman-run-container-with-systemd-PID1--2
Rebased w/ steaming-hot VM images + @giuseppe #11068 fix

[Yay, some progress (job link)](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5485998860664832).

@giuseppe any idea why this shows up on **rootless** + **crun** systems w/ my new VM images (neither prior image failed like this to my knowledge)?

- [Ubuntu 21.04 rootless + crun ](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5293373838852096/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2104-rootless-host.log.html#t--always-honor---cgroup-parent--1) - `crun-100:0.20.1-1-amd64`
- [Fedora 34 rootless + crun](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4730423885430784/html/int-podman-fedora-34-rootless-host.log.html#t--always-honor---cgroup-parent--1) - `crun-0.20.1-1.fc34-x86_64`

Hmpf, F33 system tests (local and remote) throwing [that bdflush permission denied](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5680401931829248/html/sys-remote-fedora-33-root-host.log.html) thing again.  I thought #10690 fixed this, any idea how this could be possible @rhatdan, the *only* thing I touched (I promise) is the the whole damn environment :stuck_out_tongue_winking_eye: 

(seriously, maybe I'm using an old buildah package w/o the vendored in c/common fix? - it is F33 after all)
double ugg...we don't log the buildah version...
[...found it: `buildah-1.21.4-4.fc33.x86_64.rpm` (log)](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5089366818160640?logs=main#L651)

[`sys remote fedora-33 root host`](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6097551033106432/html/sys-remote-fedora-33-root-host.log.html) is now:
```
[+0477s] not ok 151 podman diff with buildah container 
         # (in test file test/system/140-diff.bats, line 43)
         #   `buildah run buildahctr sh -c "touch /$rand_file;rm /etc/services"' failed
         # # podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_l8BI rm --all --force
         # # podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_l8BI ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
         # # podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/podman_tmp_l8BI images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
         # quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610 9f9ec7f2fdef
         # buildahctr
         # error running container: error from runc creating container for [/bin/sh -c touch /DtGkYF0Mlq;rm /etc/services]: time="2021-08-09T11:47:33-05:00" level=error msg="container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: error adding seccomp filter rule for syscall bdflush: permission denied"
         # : exit status 1
         # error while running runtime: exit status 1
```
[`int podman fedora-33 root container`](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6238288521461760/html/int-podman-fedora-33-root-container.log.html) is a huge series of:
```
Error: unable to load cgroup at /machine.slice/libpod-4b4e72996238d8d10fa4fc3c5d1a79fe57319b3d5bab98afa54daf3293d48091.scope/libpod_parent/libpod-77a6f2c4ec02cca08dd2e3938b28f3cb110ce995dac9cb6404a1f9c8acd4f5ce: cgroup deleted
```
Aye...this is actually an improvement, since most of the system-tests weren't even running this morning :grin:

> sys remote fedora-33 root host is now:

Note: I found/fixed a bug in building these VM images, that was blocking the **disable CGv2** kernel option from taking effect (in F33).  So this might be a legit problem that crept in.  In other words, the current (active on main) F33 images may actually be using CGv2 + crun :confused: (I haven't checked)

Note: Failing F33 system test (bdflush: permission denied SNAFU) seems likely due to old/outdated buildah version.  [Test code](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/system/140-diff.bats#L40-L60) + [system-test logs](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5582740348207104/html/sys-remote-fedora-33-root-host.log.html) suggest problem must be in buildah, given all other integration and system tests are passing (without the bdflush error).
I think it could be an outdated runc

> I think it could be an outdated runc

Yeah, that was suggested during our call. The VM is [running runc-1.0.1-1.fc33-x86_64](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5053686276685824?logs=package_versions#L13) which someone confirmed is correct/latest.  I'm happy to be wrong, but it seems we should see the failure in the integration tests  :confused: 

Since @lsm5 said he was doing a new buildah package update anyway, and the error in this test comes from (ancient) buildah, I thought I'd start there.  If there's a newer runc, we can certainly try that as well.
Trying new buildah package now.
Confirmed: [The buildah update](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2021-2bcbfc2b3c) **did** fix the F33 system-test failure.
New image build ref: https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/86

Summary of latest issues:

* [int podman fedora-33 root container](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5133232862658560/html/int-podman-fedora-33-root-container.log.html#t--podman-stats-with-json-output--2) - 12 failures, all/Most have error: `Error: unable to load cgroup at /machine.slice/libpod-895...a91.scope/libpod_parent/libpod-da8...be4: cgroup deleted`
* [sys podman fedora-33 root host](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4711020397592576/html/sys-podman-fedora-33-root-host.log.html) - Podman diff test failure, `error running container: error from runc creating container...rule for syscall bdflush: permission denied`.  Damnit, I thought I fixed this with the update to [buildah-1.22.0-2.fc34.x86_64](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2021-2bcbfc2b3c).  I manually tested once at it did not reproduce, so maybe this is a race and/or more complex issue?
* sys podman fedora-33 rootless host - same deal as above
* sys remote fedora-33 root host - also the same :cry:
OIC, we've ended up with `buildah-1.21.4-5.fc33.x86_64` in the images, so my attempted update failed.

@umohnani8 or @rhatdan do you have any idea why all of a sudden, all [the F33 *containerized* 'podman stats' tests are failing](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6227469633060864/html/int-podman-fedora-33-root-container.log.html) with new images?

This is not a flake, it happens 100% of the time on F33.  Notably, I ***do not*** see this failure on [the v3.3 branch (same VM images)](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11163/checks?check_run_id=3350700558).
@cevich No idea I never saw this error before. The only thing I know is that `podman stats` tests are "new". They were skipped due a bug and just got enabled two weeks ago in #11003.

> tests are "new"

Yeah, CI for that PR ran using the really (REALLY) old Fedora (not your fault) giving a false-positive for containerized F33.  Thanks for your quick response.

@mheon @umohnani8 or @rhatdan - These "new" `podman stats` tests already have a blanket:

`SkipIfRootlessCgroupsV1("stats not supported on cgroupv1 for rootless users")`

Perhaps should that be changed so the tests skip if running under a cgroupv1 container environment?

> > tests are "new"
> 
> Yeah, CI for that PR ran using the really (REALLY) old Fedora (not your fault) giving a false-positive for containerized F33. Thanks for your quick response.
> 
> @mheon @umohnani8 or @rhatdan - These "new" `podman stats` tests already have a blanket:
> 
> `SkipIfRootlessCgroupsV1("stats not supported on cgroupv1 for rootless users")`
> 
> Perhaps should that be changed so the tests skip if running under a cgroupv1 container environment?

I think you are right, see `podman pod stats`:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/edf9ec38e40bd2471c870c9bbf3b2685d5e45fa7/test/e2e/pod_stats_test.go#L21-L23


However this does not explain why they work on main right now.
Well looks like you added this to podman pod stats in  https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/8997a2d106a26fa1c643824b0c2638aedd05159a

> Well looks like you added this to podman pod stats

Correct, with the `pod` part being the key, that was known to not work at the time.  

> However this does not explain why they work on main right now.

Actually I have a (still untested) theory about that.  The old F33 images might actually be running with CGv2 and nobody noticed.  I "fixed" that issue for these new VM images, so they're **for sure** using CGv1.  Probably time I actually confirm this theory...

...confirmed:

```
[root@cevich-prior-fedora-c5348179051806720 podman]# cat /proc/cmdline
BOOT_IMAGE=(hd0,msdos1)/boot/vmlinuz-5.11.19-200.fc33.x86_64 root=UUID=0e28d03c-9307-4c5c-b31e-7b0574239ccf ro no_timer_check net.ifnames=0 console=tty1 console=ttyS0,115200n8
[root@cevich-prior-fedora-c5348179051806720 podman]# stat -f -c %T /sys/fs/cgroup
cgroup2fs
```

So, mystery solved!

Okay, trying with a skip (assuming it's okay, unless someone says otherwise).  I also added an actual check to confirm we're running CGv1 | CGv2 in the expected VMs.

@mheon @umohnani8 or @rhatdan PTAL and confirm it's okay to skip these tests in a CGv1 containerized environment.

In case it helps re: int podman ubuntu-2010 rootless host

Old (working) run:
https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5453119912935424/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2010-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-run-container-with-systemd-PID1--1

New (broken) run:

https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4573521146806272/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2010-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-run-container-with-systemd-PID1--1
Changes LGTM still, but needs a rebase.

> In case it helps re: int podman ubuntu-2010 rootless host

This appears to be a flake, as now the test has passed.  Sadly, I don't think it's the last time we'll encounter it w/o further deep investigation :disappointed:
[Re-ran `sys podman fedora-34 root host` test.](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5052793661685760)

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@cevich, is this still a WIP as the title claims?
LGTM
/lgtm
